results.rationale: sub->rects allocated at line 519 is freed via avsubtitle_free only on the missing-palette path (line 528). The other error returns inside the loop (lines 536, 547, 573, 584) return without calling avsubtitle_free, leaking sub->rects and any AVSubtitleRect entries already allocated by av_mallocz at line 532. On the success path (return 1) ownership transfers to the caller, but the inner error paths do not, confirming a feasible leak.
results.fix_suggestion: Replace the bare error returns inside the loop with cleanup that calls avsubtitle_free(sub) before returning (e.g. goto fail; label that frees sub), matching the line 528 path.

results.rationale: sub->rects is allocated at line 519. The palette-missing path (528) correctly calls avsubtitle_free, but multiple subsequent early-return error paths inside the loop return without freeing sub->rects (and the rects already stored in it): line 536 (!rect), line 547 (invalid object with AV_EF_EXPLODE), line 573 (decode_rle failure), and line 584 (palette data alloc failure). On these feasible error paths the allocated memory is never released, leaking sub->rects and any rect entries allocated so far.
results.fix_suggestion: Replace the bare error returns in the loop (lines 536, 547, 573, 584) with calls to avsubtitle_free(sub) before returning, mirroring the cleanup done on the missing-palette path.

results.rationale: The allocation at line 584 is stored in s->predictor_state[i] (priv_data). Subsequent allocations at 590 and 596 have early-return error paths (lines 591-592, 597-598) that return AVERROR(ENOMEM) without freeing the already-allocated predictor_state, tap_quant, or predictor_k. In FFmpeg, the decoder's close function is only invoked on init failure when FF_CODEC_CAP_INIT_CLEANUP is set in caps_internal. The sonic decoder historically does not set this flag, so a failure after line 584 leaks the prior allocations. Confidence is limited because the close function and codec definition were not included in the slice, so the presence/absence of FF_CODEC_CAP_INIT_CLEANUP cannot be verified directly.
results.fix_suggestion: Set FF_CODEC_CAP_INIT_CLEANUP in the sonic decoder's caps_internal so sonic_decode_close runs on init failure, or free tap_quant/predictor_k/predictor_state on each error-return path within sonic_decode_init.

results.rationale: five_planes (== dst) is allocated at line 2205 when the SEPARATED/RGBA branch is taken. It is only freed in the tmpbuf-failure branch (av_free(five_planes) at 2300) and, by ffmpeg convention, in the post-loop conversion block. The strip-decoding loop starting at 2209 contains additional error returns that do not free five_planes, so on those feasible error paths the allocation leaks. The return at 2207 is safe because the allocation failed there. The free at 2300 covers only one error case, confirming the cleanup is conditional rather than universal.
results.fix_suggestion: Free five_planes via a single cleanup/goto path (av_freep(&five_planes)) before every error return inside the strip-decoding loop, not just the tmpbuf-failure branch.

results.rationale: tx_in[]/tx_out[] are local buffers allocated at lines 151-154 and only freed at lines 186-189 on the success path. On the allocation-failure path at line 155-156, the function returns AVERROR(ENOMEM) without freeing any of the already-successful allocations (e.g., tx_out[0] from line 153 leaks if tx_out[1] at 154 fails). The av_tx contexts tx/itx are also leaked on this and the earlier 143/148 error returns, but for the reported site the calloc at 153 is not released on the feasible error path.
results.fix_suggestion: On the error path at line 155-156, goto a cleanup label that calls av_freep on tx_in[0..1]/tx_out[0..1] and av_tx_uninit on tx/itx before returning.

results.rationale: tx_in/tx_out are local buffers never stored in the context struct. On the normal path they are freed at lines 186-189, but the error check at lines 155-156 returns AVERROR(ENOMEM) without freeing any of the already-successfully-allocated buffers. If tx_in[1] (line 152) succeeds but a later allocation (153/154) fails, tx_in[0] and tx_in[1] leak. The intervening early returns at 144/149 don't free either, but only tx_in[0] onward are at risk since they're allocated after.
results.fix_suggestion: In the failure branch at line 155, av_freep all four tx_in/tx_out buffers (goto a cleanup label) before returning AVERROR(ENOMEM).

results.rationale: The four local buffers tx_in[0], tx_in[1], tx_out[0], tx_out[1] (allocated lines 151-154) are only freed on the success path at lines 186-189. The NULL check at line 155-156 returns AVERROR(ENOMEM) without freeing any of the already-allocated buffers, leaking the ones that did succeed. These buffers are function-local, owned, and not transferred, so the early return at line 156 is a feasible leak path for the allocation at line 154 and its siblings.
results.fix_suggestion: On the line 155-156 error path (and the av_tx_init failure returns at 144/149), free tx_in[0..1] and tx_out[0..1] with av_freep and uninit tx/itx before returning, e.g. via a goto fail cleanup block.

results.rationale: The local buffers tx_in[0..1] and tx_out[0..1] are allocated at lines 151-154 and freed via av_freep at lines 186-189 only on the success path. On the error path at line 155-156, if any of the four allocations fails (e.g. tx_in[0] succeeds but tx_in[1] returns NULL), the function returns AVERROR(ENOMEM) immediately without freeing the buffers that did succeed, leaking them. These are local stack pointers not stored in s, so they are unrecoverable after return.
results.fix_suggestion: On the failure branch at line 155, free any successfully allocated tx_in/tx_out buffers (e.g. goto a cleanup label that calls av_freep on all four and av_tx_uninit) before returning AVERROR(ENOMEM).

results.rationale: tx_in[0..1] and tx_out[0..1] are local stack pointers holding heap allocations (lines 151-154). On the normal path they are freed at lines 186-189, but the error check at line 155 returns AVERROR(ENOMEM) at line 156 without freeing any of the buffers that were already successfully allocated. Since these pointers are local and not stored in any persistent structure, partial allocations (e.g., tx_in[0], tx_in[1], tx_out[0] succeed but tx_out[1] fails) leak. Earlier returns at 144 and 149 also bypass these frees, though those occur before allocation. The reported site (av_calloc at 154) feeds the failure branch that leaks the sibling allocations.
results.fix_suggestion: Before returning at line 156, free the already-allocated buffers (and av_tx_uninit tx/itx) via a goto cleanup/fail label that runs av_freep on tx_in[0], tx_in[1], tx_out[0], tx_out[1].

results.rationale: av_calloc at line 151 (and 152-154) allocates tx_in/tx_out buffers. On the error path at lines 155-156, if any of the four allocations fails, the function returns AVERROR(ENOMEM) without freeing the buffers that did succeed (e.g. tx_in[0]) and without calling av_tx_uninit on tx/itx. The successful-path frees at lines 186-189 are skipped, so the line 151 allocation leaks on that feasible failure path. The earlier returns at 144 and 148 also skip cleanup, compounding the leak.
results.fix_suggestion: Add a cleanup/goto-fail path that calls av_freep on tx_in[0..1]/tx_out[0..1] and av_tx_uninit on tx/itx before each error return (lines 144, 148, 156).

results.rationale: At lines 151-154 four buffers (tx_in[0/1], tx_out[0/1]) are allocated, then line 155 checks if any is NULL and returns AVERROR(ENOMEM) at line 156 without freeing the buffers that did succeed. If, e.g., tx_out[1] (154) fails but tx_out[0] (153) succeeded, the allocation at line 153 leaks. The cleanup via av_freep at lines 186-189 only runs on the success path. The tx/itx TX contexts are also not uninited on this error return. So the flagged allocation has a feasible no-free path.
results.fix_suggestion: On the line 155-156 error path, free all successfully allocated buffers (av_freep tx_in[0/1], tx_out[0/1]) and call av_tx_uninit(&tx)/&itx before returning, e.g. via a shared error label.

results.rationale: At lines 151-154 four buffers are allocated with av_calloc. The NULL check at line 155 returns AVERROR(ENOMEM) at line 156 without freeing any of the buffers already allocated successfully (e.g. if tx_in[1] succeeded but tx_out[0] failed, tx_in[0] and tx_in[1] leak). The av_freep cleanup only runs on the success path at lines 186-189. The tx/itx TX contexts also leak on this path since av_tx_uninit is only reached at 183-184. This is a feasible memory leak on the error path; the NeverFree finding is correct even though the checker is labeled null-deref.
results.fix_suggestion: Before returning AVERROR(ENOMEM) at line 156, free the allocated buffers (av_freep on tx_in[0/1], tx_out[0/1]) and uninit tx/itx via a shared error label.

results.rationale: hann is allocated and stored in atempo->hann. All sibling allocations (frag data, xdat, correlation, buffer) are released via yae_release_buffers, which is the designated cleanup called both on the fail path (line 334) and on re-reset (line 282). The analyzer flags hann specifically among many structurally identical allocations, which indicates av_freep(&atempo->hann) is missing from yae_release_buffers, so hann leaks on every reset and at uninit. The yae_release_buffers body is not in the slice, so this cannot be confirmed with full certainty.
results.fix_suggestion: Add av_freep(&atempo->hann); to yae_release_buffers so hann is released alongside the other context buffers on reset and uninit.

results.rationale: s->table is allocated via av_malloc_array and stored in the filter's private context (ctx->priv). In af_tremolo.c there is no uninit callback freeing s->table, so the buffer allocated each time config_input runs is never released. config_input can be invoked on reconfiguration, compounding the leak. The provided slice shows no free path and no ownership transfer beyond storage in priv.
results.fix_suggestion: Add an uninit callback to the filter that calls av_freep(&s->table), and av_freep(&s->table) before reallocating in config_input to avoid leaks on reconfiguration.

results.rationale: On the allocation-failure error paths (lines 320-323 and 326-329), only the top-level array is freed via av_free(mask). The inner arrays already allocated in prior iterations (mask[0..a-1] and their sub-arrays mask[..][..]) are leaked because the cleanup never frees them. The allocation at line 319 (and 325) inside the loop is therefore not released on all feasible failure paths.
results.fix_suggestion: On allocation failure, free all previously allocated inner arrays (loop over a and b freeing mask[i][j] then mask[i]) before av_free(mask), or use a helper/goto cleanup that walks the partially built structure.

results.rationale: On the allocation-failure paths (lines 320-322 and 326-328), the code calls av_free(mask) which only releases the top-level int*** array. All previously allocated mask[a] sub-arrays and mask[a][b+a] inner rows (including those allocated at line 325 in prior iterations) are leaked. The success path stores mask in s->mask for later cleanup, but the error paths free incompletely.
results.fix_suggestion: On allocation failure, iterate and free all previously allocated mask[a][*] rows and mask[a] arrays before av_free(mask), e.g. via a dedicated cleanup loop or goto fail that walks back through allocated indices.

results.rationale: On the allocation-failure error paths the code calls av_free(mask) (lines 321 and 327), which frees only the top-level pointer. All previously allocated inner rows mask[a] and cells mask[a][b+a] (including the allocation at line 325) are leaked because av_free does not recurse. Each failed iteration after some inner buffers were allocated leaks those buffers. The success path transfers ownership via s->mask = mask (line 338), so the leak is confined to the error paths, but it is a feasible leak.
results.fix_suggestion: On error, free nested allocations before returning: loop freeing mask[i][j] then mask[i] for all already-allocated entries, then av_free(mask).

results.rationale: On the allocation-failure paths the code calls av_free(mask) which only releases the top-level pointer array. Any inner arrays already allocated in prior iterations (mask[0..a-1] and their mask[a][...] rows) are never freed, leaking the memory allocated at line 319 (and 325). The cleanup is incomplete on the error branches at lines 320-322 and 326-328.
results.fix_suggestion: On allocation failure, free all previously allocated inner arrays (loop over already-set mask[0..a-1] and their rows) before av_free(mask), or store partial results in s->mask and delegate to uninit() for full cleanup.

results.rationale: mask is a 3-level nested allocation. On allocation failure inside the loops (line 320 for mask[a] or line 326 for mask[a][b+a]), the error handlers only call av_free(mask), which frees the top-level pointer array but not the already-allocated inner arrays (mask[0..a-1], their sub-arrays, and the partially filled mask[a]). All those nested allocations leak on the reported failure path.
results.fix_suggestion: On failure, free all already-allocated inner arrays before freeing mask (e.g., loop over allocated mask[i][j] and mask[i] in a cleanup label), instead of calling av_free(mask) alone.

results.rationale: On the allocation failure path the function returns AVERROR(ENOMEM) without freeing buffers that already succeeded. If blur_data[0] and/or blur_data[1] were allocated but a later av_malloc fails, those buffers leak since no cleanup runs before the early return. The function does not call its own free routine on error, relying on the caller to invoke an uninit which is not guaranteed on init failure.
results.fix_suggestion: On the ENOMEM error path, free already-allocated buffers (e.g. call ff_vmafmotion_uninit or av_freep on blur_data[0], blur_data[1], temp_data) before returning.

results.rationale: The AVFormatContext allocated at line 1175 via avformat_alloc_context() is released on the error path with av_freep(&ast->sub_ctx) (line 1198). av_freep only frees the top-level struct pointer; it does not free the internal members that avformat_alloc_context() (and avformat_open_input() on the success-then-error path, e.g. nb_streams != 1) allocate. The correct deallocator is avformat_free_context()/avformat_close_input(). This leaks the context's internal allocations on every error branch reached after 1175.
results.fix_suggestion: Replace av_freep(&ast->sub_ctx) in the error handler with avformat_free_context(ast->sub_ctx); ast->sub_ctx = NULL; (or avformat_close_input(&ast->sub_ctx) once it has been opened) so the context's internal structures are also released.

results.rationale: av_realloc result is assigned back into the same 'nodes' variable. On allocation failure av_realloc returns NULL while the original buffer remains allocated, so the only pointer to that buffer is overwritten with NULL and leaked. data->nodes is assigned the buffer only in the success branch (line 137), so concat_close(h) in the failure branch cannot free the original buffer. This is the classic realloc self-assignment leak.
results.fix_suggestion: Use a temporary pointer for the realloc result, e.g. 'AVNode *tmp = av_realloc(nodes, ...); if (!tmp) { concat_close(h); err = AVERROR(ENOMEM); } else { nodes = tmp; data->nodes = nodes; }' so the original buffer is not lost on failure.

results.rationale: Classic realloc self-assignment leak: nodes = av_realloc(nodes, ...) overwrites the original pointer when av_realloc returns NULL on failure, losing the reference to the previously allocated buffer. On that failure branch concat_close(h) is called, but data->nodes is only assigned the new buffer in the success branch (line 137), so the old block is not reachable for freeing and leaks.
results.fix_suggestion: Use a temporary pointer for the realloc result, e.g. if (!(tmp = av_realloc(nodes, ...))) { av_freep(&nodes); concat_close(h); err = AVERROR(ENOMEM); } else { nodes = tmp; data->nodes = nodes; } so the original allocation is not lost on failure.

results.rationale: sgpd_entries is allocated at line 3310 and freed on the normal paths (3352, 3380), but the early error return at line 3333 (return AVERROR_INVALIDDATA when distance > 32) inside the OPUS branch returns without freeing sgpd_entries, leaking the buffer. This path is reachable for crafted OPUS input where preroll distance exceeds 32 packets.
results.fix_suggestion: Free sgpd_entries before the early return at line 3333, e.g. replace with: av_free(sgpd_entries); return AVERROR_INVALIDDATA; (or use a goto cleanup label).

results.rationale: sgpd_entries is allocated at line 3310 and freed on the normal paths (3352, 3380). But the early return at line 3333 (when distance > 32) returns AVERROR_INVALIDDATA without freeing sgpd_entries, leaking the buffer on that feasible path. The reported 'null_deref/PartialLeak' is really a conditional resource leak: allocation is conditionally released on some paths but not on the distance>32 error path.
results.fix_suggestion: Free sgpd_entries before the early return at line 3333, e.g. `av_free(sgpd_entries); return AVERROR_INVALIDDATA;` (or use a goto fail cleanup label).

results.rationale: av_calloc'd buffers are stored in p->frame_flags/p->frame_offsets within the demuxer priv_data, which outlives this function. FFmpeg frees priv_data itself but not heap buffers it points to; those require an explicit AVInputFormat read_close/deinit callback. The provided slice (and the per-translation-unit analysis that flagged NeverFree) shows no such free for these buffers. There is also a secondary leak at the early return on line 77: if frame_flags succeeds but frame_offsets fails, the function returns AVERROR(ENOMEM) leaving frame_flags allocated unless a close handler reclaims it. Without the close handler in the slice this matches a NeverFree pattern, though full confirmation would require seeing the demuxer struct/close function.
results.fix_suggestion: Add a read_close/deinit callback (e.g. pdv_read_close) that calls av_freep(&p->frame_flags) and av_freep(&p->frame_offsets), and register it on the demuxer. Also free already-allocated buffers before the ENOMEM return at line 77.

results.rationale: ctx->tracks is allocated with av_malloc_array at line 139, but several error paths after the allocation (lines 149, 163, 167, 187, 202) return without freeing it. In FFmpeg, when read_header returns an error, avformat_open_input goes to its fail path and calls avformat_free_context, which frees s->priv_data itself but does not free sub-allocations like ctx->tracks, and does not invoke read_close. Unless pp_bnk_read_close (not shown in the slice) is guaranteed to run on this failure path, the allocated array leaks on the truncation/validation error returns.
results.fix_suggestion: Free ctx->tracks (av_freep(&ctx->tracks)) on the error-return paths after line 139, or implement pp_bnk_read_close and ensure it is invoked on read_header failure.

results.rationale: config is heap-allocated at line 528 and ownership transferred to the caller via *out (line 535). On the 629-False (success) path the caller uses config in av_strlcatf (631-634) and breaks without a visible av_free; the error path leaks nothing since *out stays NULL. Saber flags the success continuation as a partial leak. The function tail that would normally free config is outside the slice, so confidence is moderate.
results.fix_suggestion: Free the transferred config in the caller after use (e.g. av_free(config) once av_strlcatf has copied it into buff, or at the function's common cleanup/return path).

results.rationale: av_malloc_array assigns to the out-param *frames at line 545, but the three error returns inside the parsing loop (lines 559, 566, 578) return AVERROR without freeing *frames or resetting it to NULL. A caller that treats a non-zero return as failure will typically not free the array, leaking the allocation. The function should release the buffer it allocated before returning an error.
results.fix_suggestion: On each error return after allocation, call av_freep(frames) (which frees and nulls *frames) before returning the error code.
